findings.message: The create_item mutation accepts a new required `description: str` argument but the resolver body silently drops it: it calls `repository.create_item(session, name)` and never passes `description`. The input is accepted, validated as required, then discarded — clients believe they are persisting a description that is never stored (the repository signature is `create_item(session, name)` and the Item model has no description column).
findings.suggestion: Either thread the value through (`repository.create_item(session, name, description)` plus a corresponding `description` column on the Item model and a `description` field on the `Item` GraphQL type), or remove the unused argument until the storage path supports it.

findings.message: Adding a non-null `description: str` argument to the existing create_item mutation is a breaking schema change with no compatible path. Previously-valid client operations that call `create_item(name: ...)` will now fail GraphQL validation because the new argument is required and has no default.
findings.suggestion: Make the new argument nullable with a default (`description: str | None = None`) so existing clients keep working, then deprecate/migrate before tightening to non-null.
